enrichment.short_description: Commerce Layer is a headless, API-first commerce platform that provides the backend infrastructure for carts, checkout, pricing, promotions, inventory and order management, without shipping a storefront or theme layer. Founded in 2017 by Filippo Conforti and Massimo Scardellato with roots in Prato, Italy, it is built for developers assembling composable commerce stacks across websites, mobile apps, POS systems and emerging AI-agent-driven shopping channels.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium/tiered SaaS pricing with a free entry tier for getting started, paid Standard plans, and custom-quoted Enterprise plans for higher volume and advanced features; exact tier pricing is published on Commerce Layer's own pricing page and subject to change
enrichment.starting_price: Free tier available to start building; paid plans require checking Commerce Layer's official pricing page for current figures
enrichment.company_size: Small-to-mid-size startup team (headcount not fully publicly disclosed), venture-backed with roughly 22 million USD raised across funding rounds as of public reporting
enrichment.headquarters: Founded in Prato, Italy, with a distributed/remote-friendly team and additional business presence in the United States
enrichment.seo_title: Commerce Layer Review 2026: Headless Commerce API Platform
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Commerce Layer is an API-first headless commerce platform for carts, checkout, pricing and orders. See features, pricing model and FAQs for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Commerce Layer is an API-first, headless commerce platform founded in 2017 by Filippo Conforti, who serves as CEO, and Massimo Scardellato, who serves as CTO, with roots in Prato, Italy, and subsequent expansion including a US business presence and international venture backing, having raised roughly 22 million US dollars across multiple funding rounds from investors including Benchmark and Coatue. Unlike traditional or even many modern ecommerce platforms that bundle a storefront, theme engine and admin dashboard together with the backend, Commerce Layer deliberately ships no storefront at all; it is pure commerce infrastructure delivered as a set of composable APIs covering carts, checkout, pricing, promotions, taxes, inventory, orders, shipments, customers and payments, meant to be wired into whatever frontend a business already has or wants to build, whether that is a custom React or Next.js site, a headless CMS-powered marketing site, a native mobile app, an in-store point-of-sale system, a voice assistant, or increasingly, AI shopping agents that need to programmatically browse products and complete purchases on a user's behalf. The platform advertises more than 400 API endpoints with sub-90-millisecond average response times, a 99.99 percent uptime guarantee backed by more than 60 CDN edge locations globally, and over 100 real-time webhook trigger events so that other systems (ERPs, CRMs, marketing tools) can react instantly to commerce events like a new order or a stock change. A core differentiator in Commerce Layer's positioning is multi-market and multi-currency support built in from the ground up, aimed at fast-growing, digital-first brands expanding internationally without needing to stand up separate platform instances per country or currency; this is paired with a Distributed Order Management System that gives real-time inventory visibility across multiple locations and fulfillment sources, and a promotion engine that supports both a visual rule builder and raw JSON-based configuration for more complex discounting logic. Because Commerce Layer is infrastructure rather than a full application, it is explicitly aimed at technical teams: it pairs naturally with headless content management systems and modern frontend frameworks, and Commerce Layer markets itself directly at developers building composable, best-of-breed commerce stacks rather than at non-technical merchants looking for an out-of-the-box hosted store. This makes it most comparable to other headless/composable commerce infrastructure vendors, and distinct from all-in-one platforms like Shopify or BigCommerce that include a storefront by default. Pricing follows a freemium-to-enterprise SaaS model: developers can start on a free tier to build and test integrations, with paid Standard plans and custom-quoted Enterprise plans available as usage, order volume or feature needs grow (for example, advanced multi-market configuration, premium support SLAs or higher API rate limits); exact current tier pricing is maintained on Commerce Layer's own pricing page rather than being fixed and static, so the specific dollar figures should always be checked there before quoting a customer. Support channels include an active developer community on Discord and GitHub alongside more traditional documentation and customer references, including case studies from brands such as iFIT. For a buyer's-guide audience, Commerce Layer is best suited to engineering-led ecommerce teams, digital agencies and composable-commerce consultancies building custom, high-performance storefront experiences for mid-market to enterprise brands that have outgrown the flexibility limits of monolithic, all-in-one ecommerce platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Headless Commerce Means for Commerce Layer
paragraphs: Commerce Layer ships no storefront, theme or admin-facing shopping UI. Instead it exposes commerce functionality, carts, checkout, pricing, promotions, inventory and orders, purely through APIs that a development team wires into whatever frontend, mobile app, POS system or emerging AI shopping agent they are building.

This is the defining difference from all-in-one platforms like Shopify: Commerce Layer is infrastructure meant to be assembled alongside other best-of-breed tools (a headless CMS, a search provider, a payment gateway) rather than a complete out-of-the-box store.

long_form_blocks.heading: Core Technical Capabilities
paragraphs: The platform exposes more than 400 API endpoints, reports sub-90-millisecond average response times, and offers a 99.99 percent uptime guarantee backed by more than 60 global CDN edge locations, along with real-time webhooks covering over 100 event types.

Key modules include a Distributed Order Management System for real-time multi-location inventory visibility, a universal checkout supporting multiple markets and currencies, and a promotion engine with both visual and JSON-based rule configuration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Model and Best Fit
paragraphs: Commerce Layer follows a freemium-to-enterprise pricing structure: a free tier for building and testing, paid Standard plans, and custom Enterprise quotes for higher-volume or more advanced needs; current dollar figures are published on the company's own pricing page.

The platform is best suited to engineering-led teams, agencies and composable-commerce consultancies serving mid-market to enterprise brands, particularly those expanding across multiple markets and currencies, rather than non-technical merchants wanting a turnkey hosted store.

features.title: 400+ commerce API endpoints
features.description: A broad set of REST-style APIs covering carts, checkout, pricing, promotions, taxes, inventory, orders, shipments and customers.

features.title: Universal checkout
features.description: Checkout infrastructure supporting multiple markets, currencies and payment gateways from a single integration.

features.title: Distributed Order Management System
features.description: Real-time inventory visibility and order routing across multiple fulfillment locations.

features.title: Promotion engine
features.description: A rules engine supporting both a visual builder and JSON-based configuration for discounts and promotions.

features.title: Real-time webhooks
features.description: Over 100 event triggers let external systems react instantly to commerce events like new orders or stock changes.

features.title: Multi-market and multi-currency support
features.description: Built-in support for selling across countries and currencies without separate platform instances.

features.title: POS integration
features.description: APIs support connecting in-store point-of-sale systems to the same commerce backend as online channels.

features.title: High-performance, high-availability infrastructure
features.description: Sub-90ms average API response times and a 99.99 percent uptime guarantee backed by 60-plus CDN edge locations.

pros: No forced storefront or theme layer, giving development teams full control over the frontend experience
Broad API surface covers most core commerce needs (cart, checkout, pricing, inventory, orders) without requiring multiple vendors
Strong multi-market and multi-currency support out of the box, useful for international expansion
High reported uptime and low API latency backed by a global CDN footprint
Free entry tier lowers the barrier for developers to evaluate the platform before committing
Active developer community and documentation on GitHub and Discord
cons: Requires an engineering team to build a storefront, since Commerce Layer provides no default shopping UI
Not a fit for non-technical merchants who want an out-of-the-box hosted store
Enterprise-tier pricing is quote-based and not fully transparent upfront
Composable/headless architecture generally means longer initial implementation time than an all-in-one platform
Ongoing maintenance responsibility for the frontend sits with the customer's own team or agency
Smaller ecosystem and third-party app marketplace than dominant all-in-one platforms like Shopify

faqs.question: What is Commerce Layer?
faqs.answer: Commerce Layer is an API-first, headless commerce platform providing cart, checkout, pricing, inventory and order-management infrastructure without a built-in storefront.

faqs.question: Does Commerce Layer include a storefront?
faqs.answer: No. Commerce Layer deliberately ships no storefront or theme layer; developers connect its APIs to whatever frontend, app or channel they choose.

faqs.question: Who founded Commerce Layer?
faqs.answer: Commerce Layer was founded in 2017 by Filippo Conforti (CEO) and Massimo Scardellato (CTO), with roots in Prato, Italy.

faqs.question: How much does Commerce Layer cost?
faqs.answer: It offers a free entry tier to start building, paid Standard plans, and custom-quoted Enterprise plans; exact pricing is published on the company's own pricing page.

faqs.question: Who is Commerce Layer best suited for?
faqs.answer: Engineering-led ecommerce teams, agencies and composable-commerce consultancies building custom storefronts for mid-market to enterprise brands, especially those selling across multiple countries and currencies.

faqs.question: What is a Distributed Order Management System?
faqs.answer: It is Commerce Layer's module for tracking inventory and routing orders in real time across multiple fulfillment locations.

faqs.question: Can Commerce Layer support in-store point-of-sale selling?
faqs.answer: Yes, its APIs can connect POS systems to the same commerce backend used for online and mobile channels.
